# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.
# Category: container_security

From: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/run/#runtime-privilege-and-linux-capability-settings
In Docker versions 1.5.0–2.6.4, a flag --cap-add was available to add specific capabilities when the container started. This was implemented using capabilities groups. The capability group add_capabilities is deprecated as of this version.

Starting in Docker version 17.03. The following new flags are added:


Flag	Description
--cap-add=<name>	Adds a Linux capability to the list of provided capabilities when creating the container. To find available names, run docker info --capabilities. For information on security context details for each capability name and the behavior of this flag see Security Contexts.


The flag name has the prefix cap- which is shortened to C in the man page. Docker CLI commands use the -o / --options notation. If you want to use a long option, you can also use --cap-add.
